pi_items.source: Android SharedPreferences documentation and Avare app package structure (com.ds.avare). This is the standard path where Android stores SharedPreferences for the Avare aviation GPS app.

Avare GitHub repository strings.xml resource file defines TrackUpLabel as 'Track Up'. Source code analysis shows Preferences.java contains isTrackUp() and setTrackUp(boolean) methods using this key. The key name matches the R.string.TrackUp resource identifier.

Android SharedPreferences documentation confirms boolean values are stored as lowercase 'true' or 'false' strings in XML. Avare source code shows setTrackUp(boolean trackUp) method with boolean parameter, and the task description indicates the goal is to 'Enable Track Up', which corresponds to the boolean value 'true'.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real Avare aviation GPS application data (package: com.ds.avare). The preference file path /data/data/com.ds.avare/shared_prefs/com.ds.avare_preferences.xml is the standard Android SharedPreferences location for the Avare app. The TrackUp preference key is a verified feature in Avare that controls map orientation between North Up (false) and Track Up (true) modes. When enabled (true), the map display rotates so the aircraft's heading points upward. All metadata values are accurate and match the actual implementation in the Avare source code and Android framework specifications.
